questions.question: What is the minimum age to apply for a Utah Class D driver license?
questions.explanation: Minimum age for a Utah Class D driver license is 16. The learner permit is available at 15.

questions.question: What is the minimum age to apply for a Utah entry-level learner permit?
questions.explanation: An entry-level learner permit can be obtained at age 15. A full Class D license requires age 16.

questions.question: How long may a visitor with a valid out-of-state license drive in Utah before needing a Utah license?
questions.explanation: Visitors with a valid out-of-state or out-of-country license may drive in Utah for up to 6 months.

questions.question: If you hold a learner permit from another state, may you drive in Utah?
choices: Yes, if a licensed adult is with you
No, out-of-state learner permits are not valid in Utah
Yes, but only on non-highway roads
questions.explanation: Out-of-state learner permits do NOT transfer to Utah. Full out-of-state licenses are honored for up to 6 months, but permits are not.

questions.question: Which of these makes you a Utah resident for driver licensing purposes?
choices: Visiting family in Utah for 2 weeks
Driving through Utah on a road trip
questions.explanation: You become a Utah resident if you stay 6+ months in a calendar year, register a vehicle here, obtain a Utah DL, or are gainfully employed here.

questions.question: To drive on a Utah public highway, you must have what?
choices: Nothing -- just a vehicle is enough
A valid Utah driver license, learner permit, or temporary driver license
questions.explanation: Every Utah resident driving on public highways must have a valid Utah driver license, learner permit, or temporary driver license.

questions.question: Your driver license is suspended in another state. Can you get a Utah driver license?
choices: Yes, Utah does not check other states
Yes, but only a learner permit
No, a suspension in any state blocks a Utah license
questions.explanation: You cannot have a Utah DL if your driving privilege is denied, suspended, revoked, or disqualified in Utah OR any other state.

questions.question: If you get a traffic ticket in another state, what happens to your Utah record?
choices: Nothing -- out-of-state tickets stay out of state
Utah is part of the Driver License Compact, so the conviction is forwarded to Utah
questions.explanation: Utah is a member of the Driver License Compact (DLC) and Non-Resident Violator Compact (NRVC). Out-of-state convictions get forwarded to your home state.

questions.question: A Class D license covers personal vehicles up to what weight?
questions.explanation: Class D is the regular driver license -- covers personal vehicles up to 26,000 lbs. Above that, you need a CDL class.

questions.question: A Commercial Driver License (CDL) is required for vehicles with a Gross Vehicle Weight Rating of at least what?
questions.explanation: A CDL is required for vehicles with a GVWR of 26,001 lbs or more, or designed for 16+ passengers, placarded hazmat, etc.

questions.question: A Utah Class C license is required for a single vehicle under 26,001 lbs that does what?
choices: Is registered as a farm vehicle
questions.explanation: Class C covers single vehicles under 26,001 lbs that transport 16+ occupants OR placarded hazmat. Minimum age is 21.

questions.question: Which CDL class covers a single or combined vehicle over 26,001 lbs with a towed unit over 10,001 lbs?
questions.explanation: Class B covers single/combined vehicles over 26,001 lbs with a towed unit over 10,001 lbs.

questions.question: Which CDL class is for a combined vehicle 26,001+ lbs with a towed unit over 10,001 lbs?
questions.explanation: Class A covers combined vehicles 26,001+ lbs with towed unit over 10,001 lbs -- the largest combination class.

questions.question: What license is issued to anyone under 21 who passes the Class D requirements?
questions.explanation: Anyone under 21 with a Class D license gets a Provisional license. It uses a separate point system and expires after 5 years.

questions.question: A Limited-Term Driver License is issued to whom?
choices: Non-U.S. Citizens, Nationals, or LPRs who can prove lawful presence
questions.explanation: A Limited-Term DL is issued to non-U.S. Citizens, Nationals, or LPRs who prove lawful presence. It expires when their lawful presence ends.

questions.question: What is a Driving Privilege Card (DPC) used for?
choices: Driving only -- NOT valid ID or age verification
questions.explanation: A DPC is for driving only. It is NOT valid ID or age verification, and expires every birthday.

questions.question: An interdicted driver license has a red banner that says what?
questions.explanation: An interdicted DL carries a red NO ALCOHOL SALE banner. Extreme DUI = automatic interdicted status.

questions.question: You want to legally ride a motorcycle in Utah. What endorsement do you need on your license?
questions.explanation: The M endorsement is required to operate a motorcycle. Without it, you can be fined.

questions.question: What does the letter M on a Utah license endorsement signify?
questions.explanation: M = Motorcycle endorsement. It must appear on your license to legally ride a motorcycle in Utah.

questions.question: What does the letter H on a Utah CDL endorsement mean?
questions.explanation: H = Hazardous Materials endorsement. Required to transport placarded hazmat. Requires a federal background check.

questions.question: A truck driver wants to haul placarded chemicals across Utah. What endorsement is required?
questions.explanation: The H endorsement is required for transporting placarded hazardous materials. T is doubles/triples, S is school bus, Z is taxicab.

questions.question: The T endorsement on a CDL allows you to drive what?
questions.explanation: T = Doubles/Triples endorsement (multiple trailers). Tankers use N, taxicabs use Z.

questions.question: The X endorsement on a Utah CDL combines what two endorsements?
questions.explanation: X combines Hazmat (H) and Tanker (N) endorsements. Required when you haul placarded hazmat in a tank vehicle.

questions.question: Which endorsement is required to drive a school bus in Utah?
questions.explanation: S = School Bus endorsement. P is the broader Passenger endorsement; S is school-bus specific.

questions.question: What does the Z endorsement on a Utah driver license signify?
questions.explanation: Z = Taxicab endorsement. Required to operate a taxicab commercially.

questions.question: Which letter is the Utah endorsement for a tanker vehicle?
questions.explanation: N = Tanker endorsement. X combines hazmat + tanker; T is doubles/triples; P is passenger.

questions.question: Which letter is the Utah endorsement for transporting 16+ passengers (not school bus)?
questions.explanation: P = Passenger endorsement (16+ occupants). S is the more specific school-bus endorsement.

questions.question: Restriction code B on a Utah license means what?
questions.explanation: Restriction B = corrective lenses required. You must wear your glasses or contacts when driving.

questions.question: Restriction code A on a Utah license means what?
questions.explanation: Restriction A = no restrictions. The license has no limitations on how, when, or where you can drive.

questions.question: Restriction code G on a Utah license means what?
questions.explanation: Restriction G = daylight hours only. The driver may not operate a vehicle after sunset.

questions.question: How long is an entry-level Utah learner permit valid?
questions.explanation: An entry-level learner permit is valid for 18 months. After that, you need a renewal permit if you have not yet obtained a license.

questions.question: When driving on a Utah learner permit, who must be in the front seat?
choices: A parent, guardian, instructor, or licensed adult 21+ with authorization
No one -- you may drive alone
questions.explanation: A learner permit holder must have a parent, legal guardian, approved driving instructor, OR licensed adult 21+ with parent/guardian authorization in the front seat.

questions.question: An applicant under 18 must hold a learner permit for how long before getting a license?
questions.explanation: Under 18 = 6-month permit hold required, along with completion of an approved driver education course.

questions.question: If you are 19 or older with no prior license, how long must you hold the learner permit before getting a license?
questions.explanation: Age 19+ with no prior license = 90-day permit hold (plus 40 hours practice). Driver ed is NOT required.

questions.question: An adult age 19+ without a prior license can be licensed in Utah without driver education if they do what?
choices: Hold the permit 90 days and complete 40 hours of practice driving
Pass an oral interview with DLD
Have a parent sign a waiver
questions.explanation: Age 19+ no-prior-license path: hold the permit 90 days + complete 40 hours of practice driving. Driver education is NOT required.

questions.question: What are the two main prerequisites for an under-18 driver to get a Utah license?
choices: Hold permit 30 days + pass written test
Hold permit 6 months + complete approved driver education
questions.explanation: Under 18: hold the learner permit for 6 months AND complete an approved driver education course. Both are required.

questions.question: Before getting their first license, an under-19 Utah driver must complete how many hours of practice driving?
questions.explanation: 40 hours of supervised practice driving are required, with at least 10 of those hours after sunset.

questions.question: A 16-year-old licensed Utah driver may NOT drive during which hours?
questions.explanation: Age 16 license holders have a 12 a.m. to 5 a.m. curfew, with limited exceptions (school activity, work, emergency, or accompanied by a licensed 21+ adult).

questions.question: A newly licensed 16-year-old in Utah may carry which passengers during the first 6 months?
choices: Only one friend at a time
questions.explanation: For the first 6 months of having a Utah license at age 16, only immediate family members may be in the vehicle (with limited exceptions).

questions.question: When does the nighttime curfew end for a Utah teen driver?
questions.explanation: The 12-5 curfew applies only to age-16 license holders. At 17, there is no curfew.

questions.question: What is the minimum passing score on the Utah written knowledge test?
questions.explanation: You must score at least 80% on the written knowledge test. The test is untimed and usually takes 30-45 minutes.

questions.question: How many written-test attempts are allowed per testing fee within 12 months?
questions.explanation: Three attempts per testing fee are allowed within 12 months. Maximum two written tests per day.

questions.question: What score is required on the Traffic Safety & Trends Exam?
questions.explanation: The Traffic Safety & Trends Exam is online only and requires a 100% score. Required for all first-time Class D applicants.

questions.question: What is the minimum age to take the skills test at a Utah DLD office?
questions.explanation: Skills test at the DLD: minimum age is 16. Third-party testers and high school programs can administer it at age 15.

questions.question: What is the minimum age to take the skills test with a third-party tester or high school program?
questions.explanation: Third-party testers and high school programs may give the skills test at age 15. The DLD itself requires age 16.

questions.question: On the day of your Utah skills test, who provides the vehicle?
choices: You must bring your own vehicle
questions.explanation: You must provide your own vehicle for the skills test. It must be properly registered, insured, and safe to drive.

questions.question: What can a Third-Party Tester in Utah administer?
questions.explanation: Third-Party Testers administer the SKILLS test only. The written knowledge test must be taken at the DLD.

questions.question: During the skills test, who is allowed in the vehicle?
choices: Only the applicant and the examiner
questions.explanation: Only the applicant and the examiner are allowed in the vehicle during the skills test.

questions.question: What is the Utah vision standard for getting a driver license?
choices: 20/20 in both eyes, no peripheral requirement
20/40 and 90-degree peripheral field in at least one eye
questions.explanation: Utah requires 20/40 vision AND 90-degree peripheral field in at least one eye. Glasses or contacts are allowed to meet the standard.

questions.question: At what age must drivers take a vision test with every license application or renewal?
questions.explanation: Drivers 65 and older must take a vision test with every license application or renewal.

questions.question: What kind of documents must you bring to apply for a Utah license?
choices: Original or certified copies -- no photocopies or faxes
questions.explanation: Documents must be original or certified copies. Faxed and photocopied documents are NOT accepted.

questions.question: Proof of Utah residency requires how many documents, dated within what timeframe?
questions.explanation: You must provide 2 documents proving Utah residency, each dated within 90 days. Examples: utility bill, lease, bank statement.

questions.question: If you move to a new address in Utah, how many days do you have to notify DLD?
questions.explanation: You must notify DLD of an address change within 10 days of moving. This can be done online.

questions.question: A Provisional Class D license (under 21) expires after how long? A Regular Class D (21+)?
questions.explanation: Provisional licenses expire 5 years from issue; Regular Class D licenses expire 8 years from issue. Both expire on the birthday.

questions.question: How far in advance of expiration can you renew your Utah driver license?
questions.explanation: You may renew up to 6 months before expiration. Renewing earlier is not permitted.

questions.question: Alcohol and impairing drugs reduce which four driver systems?
choices: Judgment, vision, color distinction, reaction time
questions.explanation: Alcohol and impairing drugs reduce judgment, vision, color distinction, and reaction time -- all four critical to driving.

questions.question: At what blood alcohol level (BAC) does vision begin to be impaired for all drivers?
questions.explanation: Vision is impacted at just 0.02 BAC -- well below any legal driving limit. This is part of why Utah's adult limit is set at 0.05.

questions.question: Which sense is the most important for driving?
questions.explanation: Vision is the most important sense used in driving. We get most road information visually.

questions.question: At what point does alcohol impairment begin?
questions.explanation: Impairment begins with the first drink. You do not have to feel drunk to be impaired enough to drive unsafely.

questions.question: Which categories of drugs are considered impairing drugs under Utah law?
questions.explanation: Impairing drugs include all three: illegal, over-the-counter, AND prescription. A doctor note does NOT exempt you from DUI law.

questions.question: Which of these can impair your driving?
choices: Strong emotions like anger, fear, or depression
questions.explanation: Strong emotions -- worried, excited, afraid, angry, depressed -- impair driving by distracting your mind from the road.

questions.question: Before driving on a new medication, what should you do?
choices: Take a double dose to be safe
Check the label and ask your physician about drowsiness or impairment effects
Wait until you feel any side effects
questions.explanation: Many medications cause drowsiness or impairment. Check labels and ask your physician whether it is safe to drive on a new medication.

questions.question: What is the legal BAC limit for adult drivers (21+) in Utah?
questions.explanation: Utah's adult BAC limit is 0.05 -- the lowest in the U.S. Most other states are 0.08. You can also be charged below 0.05 if impaired.

questions.question: Utah's adult DUI BAC threshold is lower than most states. What is it?
questions.explanation: Utah set its adult limit at 0.05 -- the LOWEST in the United States. Most other states are still at 0.08.

questions.question: What is the BAC limit for a Utah CDL holder while operating a commercial vehicle?
questions.explanation: CDL drivers operating commercial vehicles have a stricter 0.04 BAC limit, lower than the 0.05 personal-vehicle limit.

questions.question: What is the legal BAC limit for drivers under 21 in Utah?
questions.explanation: Under 21 = 0.00 BAC. Any measurable alcohol in your system while driving violates Utah's Not-a-Drop / zero-tolerance law.

questions.question: Can you be charged with DUI in Utah even if your BAC is below 0.05?
choices: Yes -- if you are impaired to the degree it is unsafe to drive
Only if you refuse a test
Only if you cause a crash
questions.explanation: Yes. Utah's impaired-to-the-degree law allows DUI charges below 0.05 if you are impaired enough that it is unsafe to drive.

questions.question: Under Utah law, drivers under 21 may not do which of these with alcohol?
questions.explanation: Under 21 = illegal to purchase, possess, OR consume alcohol. All three acts are prohibited.

questions.question: Under Utah's Not-a-Drop Act, a first-time under-21 offender loses driving privileges for how long?
questions.explanation: Not-a-Drop Act: under 21 with any measurable alcohol = 6-month license denial for first offense.

questions.question: A second Not-a-Drop offense within 10 years results in license denial for how long?
choices: 2 years OR until age 21, whichever is longer
questions.explanation: Second/subsequent Not-a-Drop offense within 10 years = denial for 2 years OR until age 21, whichever is longer.

questions.question: Can the Not-a-Drop Act apply to someone who has never had a driver license?
choices: No -- only license holders can be affected
Yes -- they can deny a future license
questions.explanation: Yes. Not-a-Drop applies even if you have not been issued a license. DLD can deny a future license.

questions.question: Possession or consumption of alcohol by someone under 21 in Utah results in a license suspension of:
questions.explanation: Possession/consumption (ages 13-20): 1-year license suspension first offense, 2 years for second or subsequent.

questions.question: After a Not-a-Drop suspension, what does reinstatement require?
choices: A substance-abuse assessment or Prime For Life DUI class
questions.explanation: Reinstatement after a Not-a-Drop suspension requires a substance-abuse assessment OR completion of a Prime For Life DUI class.

questions.question: How long is an adult's license suspended for a first DUI conviction in Utah?
questions.explanation: Adult first DUI conviction = 120-day license suspension. Second DUI = 2 years.

questions.question: An adult driver's second or subsequent DUI conviction results in a suspension of:
questions.explanation: Adult second/subsequent DUI = 2-year license suspension. First DUI is 120 days.

questions.question: If you are arrested for DUI in Utah, when does your administrative license suspension begin?
questions.explanation: Officers can confiscate your DL upon arrest; the administrative suspension begins on day 45 after the arrest.

questions.question: If a DUI plea is held in abeyance, does it count for future DUI charges?
choices: Yes -- it counts as a PRIOR for future DUI charges and disqualifies a CDL
questions.explanation: A plea held in abeyance does NOT show on a regular MVR, but it counts as a PRIOR for future DUI charges and disqualifies a CDL.

questions.question: Utah's Implied Consent Law means what?
choices: You consent to a vehicle search by registering
By driving, you have agreed to submit to a chemical test if a peace officer asks
You consent to traffic stops by getting a license
Only commercial drivers must take chemical tests
questions.explanation: By driving in Utah, you have already AGREED to submit to a chemical test (breath/blood/urine/oral fluids) if a peace officer asks based on suspicion.

questions.question: Under Utah's Implied Consent Law, who chooses which chemical test is given?
questions.explanation: The officer chooses which chemical test (breath, blood, urine, oral fluids) is administered. You cannot insist on a specific test.

questions.question: An adult (21+) who refuses a chemical test loses their license for how long on first refusal?
questions.explanation: Refusal (21+): 18-month revocation first offense. Second or subsequent refusal = 36 months. This is on top of any DUI charges.

questions.question: An under-21 driver who refuses a chemical test loses their license for how long on first refusal?
questions.explanation: Under-21 first refusal = 2-year revocation. Second = 36 months OR until age 21, whichever is longer.

questions.question: Do Utah's DUI laws apply to operating a motorboat?
choices: Yes -- BUI (Boating Under the Influence) = DUI, same laws apply
Only if you are commercially licensed
questions.explanation: BUI = DUI. Utah's DUI laws -- including BAC limits, implied consent, and penalties -- apply to motorboats.

questions.question: How long is the Alcohol-Restricted Driver status in Utah?
questions.explanation: Alcohol-Restricted status lasts 2, 3, 5, or 10 years depending on the offense count and type.

questions.question: Which convictions result in LIFETIME Alcohol-Restricted driver status?
questions.explanation: Lifetime Alcohol-Restricted status is triggered by automobile homicide OR a felony DUI conviction.

questions.question: If you drive with ANY measurable alcohol during an alcohol-restricted period, what happens?
questions.explanation: Driving with ANY measurable alcohol during the alcohol-restricted period = 1-year revocation.

questions.question: What is an Ignition Interlock Device (IID)?
choices: A GPS tracker on the car
A breathalyzer wired to the ignition -- car will not start if alcohol detected
questions.explanation: An IID is a breathalyzer wired to the ignition. The car will not start if alcohol is detected on the driver's breath.

questions.question: An adult (21+) convicted of a first DUI has an Ignition Interlock requirement of:
questions.explanation: Adult first DUI = 18-month IID requirement. Under-21 first DUI = 3 years.

questions.question: An under-21 driver convicted of a first DUI has an Ignition Interlock requirement of:
questions.explanation: Under-21 first DUI = 3-year IID requirement. That is longer than the adult first-DUI period (18 months).

questions.question: A second-or-subsequent DUI conviction has an IID requirement of:
questions.explanation: Second/subsequent DUI = 2-year IID restriction. Same for alcohol-related reckless driving or chemical refusal.

questions.question: A felony DUI conviction carries an IID restriction of how long?
questions.explanation: Felony DUI = 3-year IID restriction. Automobile homicide = 10 years.

questions.question: An automobile homicide conviction carries an IID restriction of how long?
questions.explanation: Automobile homicide = 10-year IID restriction. Lifetime alcohol-restricted status also applies.

questions.question: How often must an IID be monitored, and what happens if alcohol is detected?
choices: Every 60 days; failure or alcohol attempt adds 60 days to restriction
questions.explanation: IID monitoring at least every 60 days. Each failed monitoring or alcohol-detected attempt extends the restriction 60 days.

questions.question: If you drive without an installed IID while interlock-restricted, what happens?
choices: Vehicle impound + 1-year revoke + 2 more years of IID
Just an extension of the IID period
questions.explanation: Driving while interlock-restricted without an IID = impound + 1-year revocation + 2 additional years on IID.

questions.question: After a DUI arrest, what can the officer do with the vehicle?
choices: The vehicle must be released to a sober adult
The officer MAY impound the vehicle or motorboat
The vehicle must be sold immediately
Nothing -- it stays where it is
questions.explanation: Upon a DUI arrest, the officer MAY impound the vehicle/motorboat. The owner pays towing, storage, and admin fees.

questions.question: Driving with a controlled substance or its metabolite in your body in Utah results in:
questions.explanation: Mandatory revocation applies if convicted of controlled substance or metabolite in body while driving -- including prescribed meds if measurable.

questions.question: What is distraction while driving?
choices: Anything that takes your mind OR body away from driving
questions.explanation: Distraction = anything that takes your mind OR your body away from the task of driving. Phones, food, kids, GPS -- all qualify.

questions.question: Which of these is a good way to prevent distractions BEFORE you start driving?
choices: Turn off mobile devices, set GPS, eat or groom before leaving, secure loose items
Drive faster to get there before distractions occur
Bring a passenger to entertain you
Skip the seat belt to save time
questions.explanation: Pre-drive distraction prevention: turn off devices, set GPS, eat/groom before leaving, secure loose items.

questions.question: If something unexpected happens while driving (bee in car, dropped item, contact lens shifts), what should you do?
choices: Pull over immediately to handle it safely
Roll down the window and hope it leaves
questions.explanation: Pull over immediately. Do not try to handle unexpected events while still driving -- that is how serious crashes happen.

questions.question: While driving on a Utah highway, you may NOT do which of these with a wireless device?
choices: Manually text, dial, browse the internet, or take photos
questions.explanation: Utah's HWC law bans manually writing/sending/reading texts, dialing, browsing, viewing video, taking photos, or entering data while driving.

questions.question: A driver over 18 may use a wireless device while driving only how?
questions.explanation: Over-18 drivers may talk on a phone only via voice commands / hands-free. Manual dialing or texting is prohibited.

questions.question: A driver UNDER 18 may use a wireless device while driving ONLY for which purposes?
choices: Medical emergency, safety hazard, criminal activity, OR contacting parent/guardian
questions.explanation: Under 18 may only use a wireless device for medical emergency, safety hazard, criminal activity, OR contacting parent/legal guardian.

questions.question: A first texting-while-driving violation in Utah is what offense, with what maximum fine?
questions.explanation: Texting violation = Class C misdemeanor, max $100 fine. It becomes Class B if you cause injury or have a prior.

questions.question: When does a texting-while-driving offense become a Class B misdemeanor?
choices: Always -- Class B is the default
If you cause bodily injury OR have a prior texting offense
If the text was more than 140 characters
questions.explanation: Class B misdemeanor if you cause bodily injury OR have a prior texting offense. Otherwise Class C.

questions.question: Can an officer pull you over JUST for texting in Utah, with no other violation?
choices: No -- texting is a secondary offense only
Yes -- texting is a primary offense
questions.explanation: Texting is a primary offense in Utah. An officer can stop you for texting alone -- no other violation needed.

questions.question: A texting-while-driving violation in Utah may result in what additional penalty if a judge orders it?
questions.explanation: A judge may order license suspension as an additional penalty for texting violations, beyond the misdemeanor fine.

questions.question: What is aggressive driving under Utah law?
choices: Committing moving traffic offenses that endanger persons or property, OR assault with a motor vehicle
questions.explanation: Aggressive driving = committing moving traffic offenses that endanger people/property, OR assault with a motor vehicle.

questions.question: What separates road rage from aggressive driving under Utah law?
choices: Intent to endanger or intimidate another driver, in response to a roadway incident
questions.explanation: Road rage = criminal offense by a driver, in response to a roadway incident, WITH INTENT to endanger or intimidate another driver.

questions.question: A first road-rage conviction can result in what kind of license suspension?
questions.explanation: First road-rage conviction = court MAY suspend the license for 1 year. Not mandatory -- judge's discretion.

questions.question: How much sleep do most adults need per night to be alert behind the wheel?
questions.explanation: Most adults need 7 to 9 hours of sleep. Less than that, and you are at higher risk for drowsy driving.

questions.question: On a long road trip, how often should you plan to stop and rest?
questions.explanation: Plan to stop every 2 hours on long trips to stretch, refresh, and check fatigue level. Do not wait until you feel tired.

questions.question: If you must take a power nap on a long drive, how long should it be?
questions.explanation: A power nap of about 20 minutes restores alertness without the grogginess of deeper sleep.

questions.question: Which of these is a warning sign you are too drowsy to drive?
choices: Cannot remember the last few miles, drifting in lane, or hitting rumble strips
Holding the wheel with one hand
questions.explanation: Drowsy warning signs include: cannot remember the last few miles, drift in lane, hit rumble strip, missing signs, heavy eyes, constant yawning.

questions.question: Before driving on a new medicine, where can you find drowsiness warnings?
choices: The medicine label or your physician
questions.explanation: Check the medicine label and/or ask your physician. Many over-the-counter and prescription meds list 'may cause drowsiness.'

questions.question: You are 18 and just had one sip of beer at a friend's house. You decide to drive home. Are you legal?
choices: Yes -- under 0.02 is the under-21 floor
Yes -- one sip is not a real drink
questions.explanation: Under 21 = 0.00 BAC. Any measurable alcohol violates the Not-a-Drop law and could result in a 6-month license denial.

questions.question: You are 25 and blow 0.07 on a breathalyzer. You thought the legal limit was 0.08. What is true?
choices: You are under the limit -- no DUI
Utah's limit is 0.05, so you are OVER and face a first-DUI suspension of 120 days
questions.explanation: Utah's adult limit is 0.05 -- the lowest in the U.S. At 0.07 you are over. First DUI = 120-day suspension.

questions.question: You are pulled over and arrested for suspected DUI. You decide to refuse the breathalyzer to avoid evidence. What happens?
choices: Automatic license revocation: 18 months for first refusal (21+)
Required to take a urine test instead
questions.explanation: Implied consent: by driving, you already agreed to the test. Refusal = automatic revocation -- 18 months first offense for adults (21+), on top of any DUI charges.

questions.question: Caleb, 19, is convicted of his first DUI. He thinks his Ignition Interlock will be 18 months like adults. How long is it actually?
questions.explanation: Under-21 first DUI = 3-year IID restriction. That is longer than the adult first-DUI (18 months) -- Utah is stricter on young drivers.

questions.question: Mrs. Patel starts a new prescription antihistamine. The label warns of drowsiness. She drives the next morning and crashes. What is true?
choices: Prescription drugs are always legal to drive on
Impairing drugs include prescriptions; she could face DUI charges if impaired
Only illegal drugs trigger DUI law
If her doctor approved the prescription, she is immune
questions.explanation: Impairing drugs include illegal, OTC, AND prescription medications. The driver has final responsibility for knowing whether they are safe to drive.

questions.question: Sam, 16, is stopped at a red light. He pulls out his phone to text a friend 'running 5 min late.' An officer sees it. Is Sam legal?
choices: Yes -- texting at a red light is fine
Yes -- short texts do not count
No -- under-18 may only text for medical, safety, criminal, or parent reasons
questions.explanation: Under-18 HWC exceptions are: medical emergency, safety hazard, criminal activity, or parent/guardian. Texting a friend is none of those. Class C misdemeanor, max $100.

questions.question: Marisa, 22, uses her phone voice command to call her mom while driving on the freeway. They talk hands-free for 5 minutes. Is she legal?
choices: No -- phone use is illegal at any age
Yes -- over-18 may use voice commands / hands-free
Only legal if it is an emergency
questions.explanation: Over-18 drivers may use voice commands / hands-free. The ban is on manual writing, dialing, browsing, photos, or video -- voice commands are allowed.

questions.question: Tyler has been driving 6 hours straight at 2 a.m. He just hit a rumble strip twice in 10 minutes and is yawning constantly. What should he do?
choices: Drive faster to get home sooner
Pull off at the next safe place -- take a 20-minute power nap or stop for the night
questions.explanation: Multiple drowsy-driving warning signs. Pull off at the next rest area, take a 20-minute power nap, or stop for the night. Drowsy driving is treated similarly to impaired driving.

questions.question: A driver is exhausted at dusk. A deer steps suddenly into the road ahead. What should the driver do?
choices: Speed up to clear the animal
Stay in the lane and brake firmly if safe -- do NOT swerve
Swerve and brake at the same time
questions.explanation: Do NOT swerve. Stay in the lane and brake firmly if safe to do so. The risk of swerving (rollover, head-on, off-road) is worse than hitting the animal.

questions.question: Aaron, 19, is operating his family motorboat on Lake Powell. An officer asks him to submit to a chemical test. Aaron refuses. What happens?
choices: Boats are exempt from DUI law
BUI = DUI; under-21 refusal = 2-year revocation of driving privilege
Boating does not affect a driver license
Only the boating license is affected
questions.explanation: BUI = DUI. Implied consent applies. Under-21 first refusal = 2-year revocation -- and this affects his car driving privileges.

questions.question: Devin, 23, is on the Alcohol-Restricted list with an active IID restriction. He drives a friend's car (no IID) to work. He is pulled over. What is the consequence?
choices: He is fine -- the borrowed car does not count
Impound + 1-year revocation + 2 more years of IID restriction
Only the friend is in trouble
questions.explanation: Driving while interlock-restricted in a vehicle WITHOUT an IID = impound + 1-year revocation + 2 more years on IID.

questions.question: Two drivers argue at a stop sign. One follows the other, brake-checking, swerving, and forcing the other car off the road. Nobody is hurt. What charge fits?
choices: Just a traffic offense, no big deal
Road rage -- intent to endanger or intimidate
questions.explanation: Road rage = criminal offense WITH INTENT to endanger or intimidate, in response to a roadway incident. First conviction = court MAY suspend the license for 1 year.

questions.question: A flash flood has covered a low section of road. Water is about 8 inches deep and moving. You can see road markings under it. What do you do?
choices: Drive through carefully -- markings are visible
OK if you have an SUV
OK because it is only 8 inches
Turn around -- 6 inches can stall a car; never cross a flooded roadway
questions.explanation: Turn around. Even 6 inches of water can stall most cars; 2 feet can carry away an SUV. Floodwater also hides washed-out road underneath.

questions.question: Brian, 28, has the flu. He takes a nighttime cold medicine (contains a sleep aid) 2 hours before driving to a friend house at 9 p.m. What is true?
choices: Only prescription drugs trigger DUI charges
Sleep aids in cold medicine are impairing drugs -- Brian could face DUI if pulled over impaired
If you can buy it legally, you can drive on it
questions.explanation: OTC drugs absolutely count as impairing. Sleep aids in nighttime cold medicine are explicit drowsiness causers. The driver has final responsibility to know if it is safe to drive.

questions.question: You are 19 with a Provisional license. You blow 0.01 on a breathalyzer. What is the consequence under Not-a-Drop?
questions.explanation: Under 21 = zero tolerance. Any measurable alcohol triggers Not-a-Drop: 6-month license denial for first offense.

questions.question: A CDL driver operating a commercial truck blows 0.045. Are they legal?
choices: No -- CDL drivers have a stricter 0.04 limit
Yes -- CDL is the same as Class D
questions.explanation: CDL drivers operating commercial vehicles have a 0.04 BAC limit, lower than the 0.05 personal-vehicle limit.

questions.question: You are 17 and driving when you notice a serious crash off the road. Which phone use is legal?
choices: Texting your friend that you will be late
Posting a photo to social media
Calling 911 or a parent -- reporting a safety hazard qualifies
questions.explanation: Under-18 HWC exceptions: medical emergency, safety hazard, criminal activity, or parent/guardian. Calling 911 to report a crash is a safety-hazard call -- that is allowed.

questions.question: An octagon-shaped sign always means what?
questions.explanation: Octagon = STOP. The 8-sided shape is unique to stop signs so it's recognizable even when dirty, snow-covered, or obscured.

questions.question: How many sides does a STOP sign have?
questions.explanation: A stop sign has 8 sides (octagon). The shape is unique to stop signs - no other traffic sign is shaped this way.

questions.question: What does a red sign or red circle-with-slash on a sign generally mean?
questions.explanation: Red on a sign means STOP, YIELD, or PROHIBITION. A red circle with a slash means you cannot do the action shown.

questions.question: A downward-pointing triangular sign with a red border and white interior means what?
choices: Yield - slow and let other traffic go first
questions.explanation: A downward-pointing triangle (white with red border) is a YIELD sign. Slow down, stop if necessary, and let traffic with the right-of-way go first.

questions.question: A yellow diamond-shaped sign with black symbols or letters is what kind of sign?
questions.explanation: Yellow diamonds are WARNING signs - they warn of possible danger ahead (curves, intersections, slippery roads, animals, etc.).

questions.question: What color signals a general warning of possible danger ahead?
questions.explanation: YELLOW = general warning. (Orange = construction/work zone. Blue = services. Brown = recreation.)

questions.question: A fluorescent yellow-green pentagon (house-shaped) sign means what?
questions.explanation: A 5-sided fluorescent yellow-green sign marks a SCHOOL zone or school crossing. The pentagon shape is unique to schools.

questions.question: Fluorescent yellow-green signs warn drivers about what?
questions.explanation: Fluorescent yellow-green is reserved for warnings about pedestrians, schools, and bicyclists - high-vulnerability road users.

questions.question: What color marks construction and work zone signs?
questions.explanation: Orange signs (with black letters/symbols) mean construction or work zones - workers, equipment, lane changes, or detours.

questions.question: Blue signs along the highway indicate what?
choices: Motorist services like hospitals, rest areas, phones
questions.explanation: Blue signs point to motorist services - hospitals (H), rest areas, gas, food, lodging, telephones.

questions.question: Green signs on the road typically provide what kind of information?
questions.explanation: Green signs give GUIDE information - directions to cities, exit numbers, mileage - or indicate permitted movements.

questions.question: A yellow pennant-shaped sign (horizontal triangle) on the LEFT side of the road means what?
questions.explanation: The yellow pennant is the NO PASSING ZONE sign. It's always on the LEFT side of the road and marks where a no-passing zone begins.

questions.question: A white X-shaped sign with the words RAILROAD CROSSING means what?
choices: You are at a railroad crossing - stop if a train is coming
questions.explanation: A white X-shaped 'Crossbuck' marks a railroad crossing. If a train or railroad vehicle is coming, you must stop behind the stop line or before the intersection.

questions.question: A vertical white rectangle with black text (like a speed-limit sign) is what kind of sign?
questions.explanation: Vertical white rectangles with black text are REGULATORY signs - they tell you the law (speed, parking, lane use, turning).

questions.question: Brown signs on a highway typically mark what?
choices: Recreation areas, parks, or cultural sites
questions.explanation: Brown signs mark recreation, parks, historic sites, and cultural attractions.

questions.question: You see an 8-sided red sign partly covered by snow. Even before you can read the word, you know it means what?
questions.explanation: The octagonal shape is reserved exclusively for stop signs. You can identify a STOP sign by shape alone, even if the lettering is obscured.

questions.question: An upside-down triangular sign (point facing the ground) means what?
questions.explanation: A downward-pointing triangle is unique to YIELD signs.

questions.question: You're approaching a yellow diamond-shaped sign. What should you do?
choices: Slow down and prepare for possible danger ahead
Speed up to clear the area
Ignore it if no hazard is visible
questions.explanation: Yellow diamonds warn of possible danger ahead. Slow down and be prepared for what the sign is warning about.

questions.question: What does this sign mean?
choices: Stop completely and yield to traffic and pedestrians
questions.explanation: STOP. Come to a complete stop, then yield to traffic and pedestrians before proceeding.

questions.question: What does this sign mean?
choices: Slow or stop if needed and yield to other traffic and pedestrians
questions.explanation: YIELD. Slow down (or stop if needed) and let other traffic and pedestrians go first.

questions.question: What does a SPEED LIMIT sign tell you?
choices: The maximum legal speed for that road
The advisory speed for a curve
questions.explanation: A black-on-white SPEED LIMIT sign is the MAXIMUM legal speed (in good conditions). Advisory speed plaques on yellow are different.

questions.question: What does a sign showing a red circle and slash over a U-shaped arrow mean?
questions.explanation: Red circle and slash over a U-shaped arrow = NO U-TURN at this location.

questions.question: What does a sign showing a red circle and slash over a left-turn arrow mean?
questions.explanation: Red circle with a slash over a left-turn arrow = NO LEFT TURN here.

questions.question: What does a sign showing a red circle and slash over a right-turn arrow mean?
questions.explanation: Red circle with a slash over a right-turn arrow = NO RIGHT TURN here.

questions.question: What does a red square with a white horizontal bar and the words DO NOT ENTER mean?
choices: Do not enter - the road is one-way the wrong direction or otherwise closed to you
questions.explanation: DO NOT ENTER. The road ahead is one-way against you (an exit ramp or wrong-way entry). Don't drive onto it.

questions.question: You're driving and suddenly see a red rectangular sign that says WRONG WAY. What should you do?
choices: Speed up to get out of the situation
Immediately stop and turn around safely - you are driving against traffic
Ignore it if no oncoming traffic is visible
questions.explanation: WRONG WAY means you're driving against traffic on a one-way road. Stop safely and turn around immediately.

questions.question: A black horizontal rectangle with a white arrow and the words ONE WAY tells you what?
choices: Traffic moves only in the direction of the arrow
The lane is for left turns only
questions.explanation: ONE WAY: traffic flows ONLY in the direction the arrow points. Never drive the opposite way.

questions.question: A white rectangular sign that says NO TURN ON RED means what?
choices: Right turns are still allowed on red after stopping
You cannot turn right on a red light at this intersection - even after stopping
Left turns on red are prohibited
questions.explanation: NO TURN ON RED overrides the usual right-on-red-after-stop rule. You must wait for green.

questions.question: What does a yellow diamond-shaped sign with a smooth curved arrow mean?
choices: Curve in the road ahead - slow down
questions.explanation: A curved arrow on a yellow diamond means a CURVE is ahead. Reduce speed before entering the curve.

questions.question: What does a yellow diamond with a sharp right-angle arrow mean?
choices: Sharp turn ahead - reduce speed significantly
questions.explanation: A sharp right-angle arrow means a SHARP TURN (not a gentle curve). Slow down more than for a curve sign.

questions.question: What does a yellow diamond showing two arrows converging mean?
choices: Merging traffic - another lane is joining your road
questions.explanation: Two arrows merging means MERGING TRAFFIC. Be prepared for vehicles entering from the side.

questions.question: What does a yellow diamond showing one of two lanes ending mean?
choices: Lane ends - merge into the other lane
Merging traffic from a side road
questions.explanation: LANE ENDS means your lane is going away. Merge into the remaining lane safely.

questions.question: What does a yellow diamond showing a road splitting into two divided lanes mean?
choices: Divided highway begins - opposing lanes are separated by a median
questions.explanation: DIVIDED HIGHWAY BEGINS: the road ahead has a median separating directions of travel. Stay right.

questions.question: What does a yellow diamond with the symbol of a STOP sign on it mean?
choices: A stop sign is ahead - be prepared to stop
questions.explanation: STOP AHEAD warns that a stop sign is coming. Begin slowing so you can stop at the actual stop sign.

questions.question: A yellow diamond showing a traffic signal symbol means what?
choices: A traffic signal is ahead - be prepared for it
questions.explanation: SIGNAL AHEAD: a traffic light is coming. Be ready to stop if the light changes.

questions.question: What does a yellow diamond showing a person walking mean?
choices: Pedestrian crossing - watch for people crossing the road
questions.explanation: Walking-person silhouette on a yellow diamond = PEDESTRIAN CROSSING. Slow and watch for people.

questions.question: What does a yellow diamond with a bicycle symbol mean?
choices: Bicycle crossing - watch for bicyclists crossing the road
questions.explanation: Bicycle symbol on a yellow diamond = BICYCLE CROSSING. Slow and watch for riders.

questions.question: What does a yellow diamond with a leaping deer mean?
choices: Deer crossing area - watch for animals on or near the road
questions.explanation: Leaping deer on yellow = DEER CROSSING. Animals may cross unexpectedly, especially at dawn and dusk.

questions.question: What does a yellow diamond showing a car with wavy skid lines behind it mean?
choices: Slippery when wet - the road may be slick in rain or ice
questions.explanation: SLIPPERY WHEN WET. Reduce speed and avoid sudden braking or steering when the road is wet.

questions.question: What does a yellow diamond showing a truck on a downward slope mean?
questions.explanation: Truck-on-slope symbol = STEEP HILL or downgrade. Shift down and watch your speed.

questions.question: A small yellow square sign with mph numerals attached below a curve warning sign tells you what?
choices: The legal speed limit through the curve
The advisory (recommended) speed for that curve - safe speed under good conditions
questions.explanation: Yellow advisory-speed plaques aren't legally enforceable speed limits, but they tell you the safe speed for the upcoming curve or condition.

questions.question: A round YELLOW sign with a black X and the letters RR means what?
choices: Railroad crossing ahead - slow down, look, and listen for a train
questions.explanation: Round yellow RR sign = RAILROAD CROSSING AHEAD (advance warning). Slow down, look, listen, be prepared to stop.

questions.question: A fluorescent yellow-green pentagon showing two walking children means what?
questions.explanation: Two-child silhouette on the pentagon school sign = SCHOOL CROSSING. Watch for children and reduce speed.

questions.question: What does an ORANGE diamond with the words ROAD WORK AHEAD mean?
choices: Construction or maintenance work zone - slow down and watch for workers
questions.explanation: ROAD WORK AHEAD warns of an active work zone. Slow down, don't tailgate, watch for workers - and fines are doubled.

questions.question: What does an orange diamond showing a person holding a flag mean?
choices: A flagger is directing traffic ahead - obey their signals
questions.explanation: FLAGGER AHEAD. Obey the flagger's directions - they OVERRIDE signs and signals in the work zone.

questions.question: A blue square with a large white capital H on it means what?
questions.explanation: Blue H = HOSPITAL nearby. Follow the direction indicated to reach the hospital.

questions.question: A blue square with a picnic-table symbol on it indicates what?
questions.explanation: Picnic table on blue = REST AREA. Public roadside facility with restrooms, parking, and usually picnic spots.

questions.question: A red, white, and blue shield-shaped sign with a number indicates what?
questions.explanation: The red-white-blue shield is the INTERSTATE highway marker. The number tells you which interstate (I-15, I-80, I-70 in Utah).

questions.question: A white heraldic-shield-shaped sign with a black number indicates what?
questions.explanation: The plain white shield is the U.S. ROUTE marker (US-89, US-6, etc.) - a federally-numbered highway that isn't an interstate.

questions.question: What does a yellow horizontal pennant (sideways triangle) on the LEFT side of the road mean?
questions.explanation: The yellow pennant on the LEFT shoulder marks the start of a NO PASSING ZONE. It is the only sign normally posted on the left side of a two-way road.

questions.question: What does a black rectangular sign with a down-right arrow and the words KEEP RIGHT mean?
choices: Stay to the right of the median or obstruction ahead
questions.explanation: KEEP RIGHT tells you to pass an obstruction or median on the right side.

questions.question: A white rectangle with a red circle and slash over the letter P, and the words NO PARKING ANY TIME, means what?
choices: No parking is allowed at any time at this location
questions.explanation: NO PARKING ANY TIME - the red slash over P means no parking, ever.

questions.question: An orange diamond sign that says DETOUR means what?
choices: The road ahead is open but slow
Follow an alternate route around a closed road or work zone
questions.explanation: DETOUR sends you on an alternate route because the regular road is closed.

questions.question: An 8-sided red sign with white letters means what?
choices: Yield - slow and let others go
STOP - come to a complete stop and yield
questions.explanation: Octagonal red with white letters is the STOP sign. Come to a complete stop, then yield to traffic and pedestrians.

questions.question: A downward-pointing triangular sign with a red border and white interior means what?
choices: You have the right of way
Slow down or stop if needed and yield to other traffic
questions.explanation: Triangle with red border = YIELD. Slow down and let traffic with the right-of-way go first.

questions.question: A white vertical rectangle with the words SPEED LIMIT 35 means what?
choices: The minimum speed you must drive
The maximum legal speed for that road in normal conditions
questions.explanation: Black-on-white SPEED LIMIT signs are the MAXIMUM legal speed under good conditions. In bad weather, the basic-speed law still applies.

questions.question: A white square showing a red circle and slash over a U-shaped arrow means what?
questions.explanation: U-shaped arrow with red slash = NO U-TURN at this intersection or location.

questions.question: You see a red square sign with a white horizontal bar. What is its message?
choices: Do not enter - the road is one-way the wrong direction
questions.explanation: DO NOT ENTER (red with white bar). This is the front side of a one-way road, or an exit ramp - don't drive onto it.

questions.question: A red rectangular sign reading WRONG WAY means what?
choices: You are headed against traffic flow - stop and turn around immediately
questions.explanation: WRONG WAY: you've entered a one-way road going the wrong direction. Stop safely and turn around.

questions.question: A black horizontal rectangle with white letters reading ONE WAY and an arrow means what?
choices: Traffic moves only in the direction of the arrow
questions.explanation: ONE WAY: drive only in the direction the arrow points.

questions.question: What does a sign reading NO TURN ON RED mean at an intersection?
choices: Right turn on red is still allowed after stop
Right turn on red is prohibited at this intersection
All turning is banned even when green
Only emergency vehicles may turn on red
questions.explanation: NO TURN ON RED: even after coming to a complete stop, you cannot turn on red. Wait for green.

questions.question: A round yellow sign with a black X and the letters RR means what?
choices: Railroad crossing ahead - slow down, look, and listen
questions.explanation: Round yellow RR = ADVANCE WARNING that a railroad crossing is ahead. Slow, look, listen, be ready to stop.

questions.question: A white X-shaped sign with the words RAILROAD CROSSING at the actual track location means what?
choices: You are at the tracks - yield to any train and do not stop on the tracks
Trains have a separate crossing nearby
Speed up to clear the crossing
questions.explanation: The white X CROSSBUCK is at the crossing itself. Yield to any train (they always have right-of-way), and never stop your vehicle on the tracks.

questions.question: A fluorescent yellow-green pentagon (5-sided sign) with two child silhouettes means what?
questions.explanation: Yellow-green pentagon with children = SCHOOL CROSSING. Slow down and watch for kids.

questions.question: An orange diamond with a worker silhouette holding a flag means what?
choices: A flagger is controlling traffic ahead - obey them
questions.explanation: FLAGGER AHEAD. You must obey the flagger - their directions OVERRIDE signs and signals.

questions.question: An orange diamond reading ROAD WORK AHEAD tells you what?
choices: An active work zone is coming - slow down, watch for workers
questions.explanation: ROAD WORK AHEAD = active work zone. Slow down, don't tailgate, watch for workers - and remember fines are doubled.

questions.question: A yellow diamond with a smooth curved arrow means what?
choices: A curve in the road ahead - reduce speed
questions.explanation: Curved arrow on yellow = CURVE AHEAD. Slow down before entering it.

questions.question: A yellow diamond with a walking-person symbol means what?
choices: Pedestrian crossing - watch for people crossing the road
questions.explanation: Walking person on yellow = PEDESTRIAN CROSSING. (School crossing is a yellow-green PENTAGON - same idea but different shape and color.)

questions.question: A yellow pennant-shaped sign (horizontal triangle pointing right) located on the LEFT shoulder of a two-lane road means what?
choices: No passing zone - you cannot pass other vehicles here
questions.explanation: The yellow pennant on the LEFT shoulder marks the start of a NO PASSING ZONE. Stay in your lane.

questions.question: A yellow diamond sign showing a small red-and-white octagon symbol means what?
choices: A stop sign is ahead - prepare to stop
questions.explanation: STOP AHEAD: a stop sign is coming. Start slowing now so you can stop at the actual sign.

questions.question: A yellow diamond showing a traffic-light symbol means what?
choices: A traffic signal is ahead - prepare for it
questions.explanation: SIGNAL AHEAD: a traffic light is coming. Be ready to stop if the light is red or changes.

questions.question: A yellow diamond showing a car with wavy skid lines means what?
choices: Slippery when wet - the road may be slick
questions.explanation: SLIPPERY WHEN WET. Reduce speed, avoid hard braking, and steer gently when the surface is wet.

questions.question: A yellow diamond showing a truck on a steep downward slope means what?
choices: A steep downhill grade is ahead - control your speed
questions.explanation: Truck on a slope = STEEP HILL / DOWNGRADE. Downshift, brake before you need to, and watch your speed.

questions.question: A blue square with a white H means what?
questions.explanation: Blue H = HOSPITAL nearby.

questions.question: A blue square showing a picnic-table symbol means what?
choices: Rest area - public restrooms and parking
questions.explanation: Picnic table on blue = REST AREA.

questions.question: What is the difference between a CURVE sign and a TURN sign?
choices: A TURN sign indicates a sharper change in direction than a CURVE sign
TURN means right, CURVE means left
questions.explanation: TURN = sharper (closer to 90 degrees), reduce speed more. CURVE = gentler, reduce speed somewhat. Both are yellow diamonds.

questions.question: What does a yellow diamond showing an S-shaped (winding) arrow with multiple curves mean?
questions.explanation: Winding/S-shape on yellow = WINDING ROAD. Multiple curves are coming - reduce speed and stay alert.

questions.question: What does a yellow diamond showing a reverse (double-bend) arrow mean?
choices: Reverse turn - the road bends one way then the opposite
questions.explanation: Reverse turn/curve: the road bends in one direction and then the other in quick succession. Reduce speed.

questions.question: What does a yellow diamond showing two divided lanes merging back together mean?
choices: Divided highway ends - lanes will combine; two-way traffic resumes
questions.explanation: DIVIDED HIGHWAY ENDS: the median is going away and you will soon have oncoming traffic again. Move right.

questions.question: What does a yellow diamond showing a + (plus) symbol indicate?
questions.explanation: Plus-symbol on yellow = CROSSROAD AHEAD. Be alert for cross traffic at an intersection.

questions.question: A yellow diamond with a T-symbol means what?
choices: T-intersection - the road ends ahead and you must turn left or right
questions.explanation: T-INTERSECTION: the road dead-ends into a cross street. Plan to turn left or right.

questions.question: A yellow diamond with a Y-symbol means what?
choices: Y-intersection - the road splits ahead into two roads
questions.explanation: Y-INTERSECTION: the road forks into two. Pick your direction.

questions.question: What does a yellow diamond with a T-shape where a side road enters from the right mean?
choices: Side road - another road joins from the right
questions.explanation: SIDE ROAD: a smaller road joins yours from the side. Watch for vehicles entering or exiting.

questions.question: A yellow diamond showing two parallel arrows with a new lane joining on the right means what?
choices: Added lane - a new lane joins from the right (no merging required)
questions.explanation: ADDED LANE: a new lane joins from the side. You don't need to merge - both lanes continue.

questions.question: What does a yellow diamond with TWO opposing arrows (one up, one down) mean?
choices: Two-way traffic ahead (you've been on a one-way and oncoming traffic begins)
questions.explanation: TWO-WAY TRAFFIC: a one-way road becomes two-way. Stay right - oncoming vehicles are now possible.

questions.question: A yellow diamond showing two opposing arrows (left and right) means what?
choices: Double arrow - you may pass on either side of an obstruction
questions.explanation: Double arrow = you may pass an obstruction (like a median or island) on EITHER side.

questions.question: What does a yellow vertical rectangle with a single black chevron pointing in a direction mean?
choices: Chevron - sharp change in roadway direction; follow the chevron
questions.explanation: Chevron signs are placed in series along sharp curves to show drivers where the road is going. Follow them.

questions.question: Which sign uses the pentagon (5-sided, house) shape with fluorescent yellow-green color?
questions.explanation: The pentagon shape is reserved for SCHOOL zones and school crossings. Diamonds are for pedestrians and bicyclists generally.

questions.question: A pentagon-shaped sign is fluorescent yellow-green for what type of warning?
questions.explanation: Fluorescent yellow-green + pentagon = SCHOOL CROSSING. The unique color and shape highlight where children may be present.

questions.question: You see a yellow diamond with mph numerals like '35' on a smaller yellow square plaque beneath. The smaller plaque means what?
choices: Speed limit through the curve is 35 mph (legal limit)
Recommended (advisory) safe speed for the curve is 35 mph
questions.explanation: Advisory speed plaques (yellow with mph) are RECOMMENDED safe speed - not the legal limit, but ignore them at your peril.

questions.question: You want to make a U-turn at an intersection where you see a sign with a red circle and slash over a U-shaped arrow. What can you do?
choices: Make the U-turn anyway if no traffic is coming
Wait until the light turns green and then U-turn
Do not make a U-turn here - find another intersection
Make a left turn instead of a U-turn
questions.explanation: The NO U-TURN sign means U-turns are prohibited at THIS location. Find another intersection where U-turns are allowed.

questions.question: You see a one-way arrow sign pointing to the RIGHT. What does this mean for you?
choices: You may turn either direction at the intersection
Traffic on this road only flows to the right; do not drive left
Yield to traffic from the right
questions.explanation: ONE WAY arrow pointing right means traffic only flows right. Driving the opposite direction is a wrong-way driver violation.

questions.question: You're driving and see a DO NOT ENTER sign at the entrance to a road. What does it indicate?
choices: The road is closed for construction
The road is one-way against you, or you are approaching an exit-only ramp
questions.explanation: DO NOT ENTER usually means a one-way road or an exit ramp that's not meant for entry from your direction. Find another route.

questions.question: You suddenly see a WRONG WAY sign as you start down a road. What is the safest correct action?
choices: Continue cautiously to the next intersection
Speed up to clear the area quickly
Pull over safely and turn around as soon as possible - you are driving against traffic
questions.explanation: WRONG WAY: you've entered a one-way road in the wrong direction. Pull over, turn around safely - head-on crashes are deadly.

questions.question: You're stopped at a red light and want to turn right. You see a NO TURN ON RED sign. What can you do?
choices: Turn right after a complete stop
Wait for the green light - the sign prohibits right turns on red here
Turn right only if no pedestrians are present
Turn right only if the light has been red for 30+ seconds
questions.explanation: NO TURN ON RED overrides Utah's normal right-on-red-after-stop rule. You must wait for the green.

questions.question: A NO PARKING ANY TIME sign (red circle and slash over P) is posted. What can you do?
choices: Park briefly to drop someone off
Do not park at this location at any time
questions.explanation: NO PARKING ANY TIME means parking is prohibited 24/7. Even quick stops can be ticketed.

questions.question: You approach a median or obstruction and see a KEEP RIGHT sign with a down-right arrow. What do you do?
questions.explanation: KEEP RIGHT directs you to go to the right of the obstruction (median, divider, gore area).

questions.question: You see an orange DETOUR sign with an arrow. What does it mean?
choices: Follow the alternate route shown by the arrow because the regular road is closed
questions.explanation: DETOUR sends you on an alternate path around closed road. Follow the arrows to stay on the detour route.

questions.question: You arrive at a stop sign at an intersection with no other traffic in sight. What must you do?
choices: Slow down and continue if nothing is coming
Come to a complete stop, then proceed when safe
Stop only if a pedestrian is present
questions.explanation: Stop signs require a COMPLETE stop, even if no one else is around. Then proceed when safe.

questions.question: You approach a YIELD sign at a freeway on-ramp. What should you do?
choices: Slow down, find a gap, and merge - stop only if you cannot find a gap
Drive at full speed onto the freeway
Wait at the yield sign until told to go
questions.explanation: YIELD: slow down and merge when there is a safe gap. Stop only if needed to avoid a collision.

questions.question: What does this sign mean?
choices: Stop completely - 4-way, 2-way, or full stop intersection
questions.explanation: STOP - octagonal red. Complete stop required, then proceed when safe.

questions.question: What does this sign mean?
choices: Yield - slow down and let traffic with right-of-way go first
questions.explanation: YIELD - downward triangle with red border. Slow down, stop if needed, and let other traffic and pedestrians go first.

questions.question: What does this sign mean?
choices: Divided highway ends - one road resumes
questions.explanation: DIVIDED HIGHWAY ENDS - two separated lanes are about to combine into a single roadway with two-way traffic.

questions.question: On a two-lane road, you see a yellow pennant-shaped sign on the LEFT side. What must you do?
choices: Pass quickly while you still can
Do not pass any vehicles - you've entered a no-passing zone
questions.explanation: Yellow pennant on the LEFT = NO PASSING ZONE begins. Stay in your lane until the zone ends (marked by pavement striping).

questions.question: Speed-limit, parking, lane-use, and turn-restriction signs are usually what colors?
choices: White with black, red, or green letters and symbols
questions.explanation: Regulatory signs are usually white with black/red/green text or symbols. They tell you the rules.

questions.question: Horizontal rectangular signs in green or blue typically provide what?
choices: Guide or service information (directions, exits, services)
questions.explanation: Horizontal rectangles in green/blue = GUIDE or SERVICE info. Green = directions/exits, blue = services like hospitals/rest areas.

questions.question: What does this sign mean?
choices: No left turn at this location
questions.explanation: NO LEFT TURN. Find another intersection where left turns are allowed.

questions.question: What does this sign mean?
choices: No right turn on red - must wait for green
questions.explanation: NO RIGHT TURN ON RED. Even after stopping, you must wait for green to turn.

questions.question: What does this sign mean?
choices: Do not enter - the roadway is closed to you
questions.explanation: DO NOT ENTER: don't drive onto this road. It's a one-way against you or an exit ramp.

questions.question: What does this sign mean?
choices: Wrong way - you are driving against traffic on a one-way road; stop and turn around
questions.explanation: WRONG WAY: red rectangular sign indicating you've entered a one-way road in the wrong direction. Turn around immediately and safely.

questions.question: Yellow pavement lines separate traffic moving in which direction?
questions.explanation: YELLOW = OPPOSING. Yellow lines separate traffic going in opposite directions. (White = same direction.) This is the DLD's most-flagged pavement fact.

questions.question: White pavement lines separate traffic moving in which direction?
questions.explanation: WHITE = SAME direction. White lines separate lanes of traffic that flow the same way.

questions.question: You're on a two-way road and you see a single solid YELLOW line on your left. What does that tell you?
choices: Traffic on the other side of that line is going the OPPOSITE direction; passing is restricted
The shoulder is on the left
questions.explanation: Yellow always = opposing-direction traffic. The marking type (solid, dashed, double) tells you whether passing is allowed.

questions.question: You're driving on a multi-lane road and see WHITE lines between lanes. What do they indicate?
choices: Lanes going in the same direction as you
The edge of the road only
questions.explanation: White lines separate same-direction lanes. The line style tells you whether you can change lanes.

questions.question: Which statement is TRUE about line color on the road?
choices: Yellow = opposing direction, white = same direction
Color has no meaning - only the line pattern matters
questions.explanation: Yellow = opposing, white = same. Memorize this - it's tested almost every time.

questions.question: The line marking the RIGHT edge of the road is which color?
questions.explanation: The right edge line is WHITE because there's no opposing-direction traffic to your right.

questions.question: On a two-way undivided road, the LEFT edge of your lane (toward the center of the road) is marked by what color?
questions.explanation: The CENTER line on a two-way road is YELLOW because oncoming traffic is on the other side.

questions.question: You see a road with white dashed lines between two lanes. The traffic in both lanes is going which way?
questions.explanation: Dashed white = same direction. The dashing tells you lane changes are permitted.

questions.question: A two-lane road has a solid yellow center line with a dashed yellow line ON YOUR SIDE. What does this mean?
choices: Passing is permitted in your direction; passing is not permitted from the other direction
Passing is prohibited in your direction; oncoming traffic can pass
Stay in the right lane only
questions.explanation: Broken (dashed) line on YOUR side = you can pass when safe. The solid yellow on the other side means oncoming traffic cannot pass.

questions.question: Solid yellow on the OTHER (oncoming) side of the road only means what for you?
choices: Oncoming traffic cannot pass, but you can if a broken line is on your side
The road is a no-passing zone
questions.explanation: The line on your side controls YOU. If your side is dashed, you can pass. The solid yellow on the other side restricts the OTHER direction.

questions.question: The handbook calls one pavement rule the 'flagship' fact for the DLD test. What is it?
choices: Yellow lines separate opposing traffic; white lines separate same-direction traffic
questions.explanation: DLD specifically flags this rule: yellow = opposing, white = same. Confusing the two is the #1 test miss.

questions.question: True or false: A line between you and oncoming traffic can be white.
choices: False - between you and oncoming traffic the line is always yellow
questions.explanation: In Utah and across the U.S., yellow is reserved for separating opposite-direction traffic. White means same direction.

questions.question: You're approaching an unfamiliar road. A pavement line ahead is bright yellow. What do you instantly know?
choices: Oncoming traffic is on the other side of that line
questions.explanation: Yellow always tells you opposing-direction traffic is on the other side.

questions.question: On a one-way street, all pavement lines (between lanes and at the edges) should be what color?
questions.explanation: A one-way street has no opposing-direction traffic, so all lines (between lanes and on both edges) are WHITE.

questions.question: The DLD sample test asks about yellow vs white lines. Which is the correct answer to 'yellow lines separate traffic moving in __ directions'?
questions.explanation: Yellow = opposing (opposite). Memorize the pair: yellow = opposite, white = same.

questions.question: A broken (dashed) line on YOUR side of the yellow center stripe means what?
choices: Passing is permitted when safe to do so
questions.explanation: Broken/dashed line on your side = passing PERMITTED (when it's safe to do so).

questions.question: A solid yellow line on YOUR side of the center stripe means what?
choices: Passing is prohibited in your direction
You may pass on the shoulder
The lane is for trucks only
questions.explanation: Solid yellow on your side = passing PROHIBITED in your direction. The line on YOUR side controls YOUR right to pass.

questions.question: Double solid yellow lines down the center of a road mean what?
questions.explanation: Double solid yellow = NO PASSING in either direction. Both sides of traffic are restricted.

questions.question: You approach a section of road where the center line goes from a single dashed yellow line to a double solid yellow line. What is happening?
choices: A no-passing zone is starting - you cannot pass for both directions
questions.explanation: Double solid yellow marks a no-passing zone - usually because of limited visibility (hill, curve, intersection).

questions.question: On a two-lane road you see a solid yellow line on YOUR side and a dashed yellow on the OTHER side. What is your situation?
choices: You may pass; the other direction may not
You may NOT pass; oncoming traffic IS allowed to pass when their side is broken
questions.explanation: Your line is solid - you can't pass. Their line is dashed - they can pass when safe. The line on YOUR side controls YOU.

questions.question: Double solid WHITE lines (such as those separating an express/carpool lane from general lanes) mean what?
choices: Crossing is PROHIBITED - do not cross the double white line
questions.explanation: Double solid white lines = crossing PROHIBITED. On Utah's I-15 these separate the express/carpool lanes - you can only enter/exit at marked access points (broken white lines).

questions.question: On I-15 you're in the express (carpool) lane. The lane is separated by a double white line. When can you move out of it?
choices: Only at access points - sections where the line becomes dashed (broken) white
questions.explanation: You can ONLY enter or exit the express lane at marked access points (broken/dashed white lines). Crossing the double white is illegal.

questions.question: Exception: when can you cross a double white line out of the express lane?
choices: When followed by an emergency or police vehicle with flashing lights, when safe to do so
questions.explanation: Exception: if an emergency vehicle is behind you with flashing lights, you may cross the double white line right out of the express lane when safe.

questions.question: An access point in the express lane is identified by what pavement marking?
questions.explanation: Access points use BROKEN (dotted) white striping instead of the solid double white - signaling where it's legal to enter or exit the express lane.

questions.question: You want to pass a slow-moving vehicle. You glance at the center line. Which marking would tell you it's legal to attempt the pass?
choices: Yellow X painted on the road
questions.explanation: Broken yellow on YOUR side means you may pass when safe. Solid yellow on your side means no.

questions.question: Which of these means PASSING IS PROHIBITED for you?
questions.explanation: Solid yellow on your side = passing prohibited (in your direction).

questions.question: A broken (dashed) WHITE line between two lanes going the same direction means what?
choices: Lane change/passing is permitted when safe
The lane is for buses only
questions.explanation: Broken white between same-direction lanes = lane change permitted when safe.

questions.question: A SOLID white line between two lanes going the same direction means what?
choices: Lane change is discouraged; cross only if necessary
questions.explanation: A single solid white line discourages lane changes (cross only if needed). A DOUBLE solid white prohibits crossing entirely.

questions.question: You're on a two-lane road approaching a hill. The yellow center line transitions from broken to solid on your side. What's happening?
choices: A no-passing zone is starting - visibility is limited over the hill
questions.explanation: A broken-to-solid transition on your side means a no-passing zone is starting - usually because of a hill, curve, or intersection where visibility is limited.

questions.question: On YOUR side, solid yellow line + broken yellow line on the OTHER side means what?
choices: You can pass; oncoming traffic cannot
You CANNOT pass; oncoming traffic CAN pass
questions.explanation: Your side controls you. Solid on your side = you can't pass. The dashed line on the OTHER side allows oncoming traffic to pass.

questions.question: What is the rule for double solid yellow lines?
choices: You may cross to make a left turn into a driveway or side road (when safe), but you cannot pass
Crossing is permitted only at intersections
questions.explanation: You may cross a double yellow to make a left turn into a driveway/road (when safe), but you cannot use them to PASS another vehicle.

questions.question: You need to make a left turn into a driveway across a double solid yellow line. Can you?
choices: No - the double yellow forbids any crossing
Yes - you may cross a double yellow to turn left into a driveway or side road when safe
questions.explanation: Double yellow lines prohibit PASSING but you can still cross them to make a left turn into a driveway or side road, when safe.

questions.question: On a divided highway with a median, the left edge line of your roadway (on the side closest to the median) is what color?
questions.explanation: On a divided highway, the left edge of your roadway (next to the median) is YELLOW because the lanes across the median are going the opposite direction.

questions.question: The right edge line of any road (separating the travel lane from the shoulder) is what color?
questions.explanation: The right edge is always WHITE - there's no opposing-direction traffic to your right.

questions.question: If you drift over the right white edge line, you've drifted onto what?
choices: The shoulder or onto the side of the road
questions.explanation: Crossing the right white edge line means you've left the travel lane and are on the shoulder. Steer gently back - don't jerk the wheel.

questions.question: A bike lane on the right side of the road is marked by what?
choices: A solid white line plus a bicycle symbol painted on the pavement
questions.explanation: Bike lanes are usually marked with a solid white line and a bicycle symbol painted on the pavement.

questions.question: Generally, can you drive your car in a marked bike lane?
choices: Yes - bike lanes are for everyone
No - bike lanes are for bicyclists. You may briefly cross only to make a turn or enter/exit a driveway
Yes - if no bike is present
questions.explanation: Don't drive in a bike lane. You may briefly cross it to make a turn or enter/exit a driveway, but driving in it is illegal.

questions.question: Which of these allows you to cross a double yellow line legally?
choices: Making a left turn into a driveway or side road when safe
questions.explanation: You may cross a double yellow to turn into a driveway or side road (when safe). You cannot cross to pass another vehicle.

questions.question: A shared center two-way left-turn lane is marked by what?
choices: Solid yellow line on the outside and broken yellow line on the inside - on both sides of the lane
questions.explanation: The center two-way left-turn lane has a solid yellow line on the outside and a broken yellow line on the inside - on both sides. Only for left turns, not for through travel or passing.
