questions.explanation: Over 80 percent of work-zone fatalities/injuries are drivers, passengers, and pedestrians - NOT workers. Handbook p. 68.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a typical way to identify a work zone?
questions.explanation: Work zones are identified by orange signs, orange devices, flashing lights, and highly visible worker clothing - not red flares. Handbook p. 59.

questions.question: Speeding fines in a work zone are doubled because:
choices: Speeding endangers workers and other drivers in the zone
questions.explanation: The double-fine law deters speeding through zones where workers and drivers face elevated risk. Handbook p. 59, 68.

questions.question: You enter a work zone with reduced speed signs, and traffic is moving 10 mph above the posted work-zone limit. What should you do?
choices: Match traffic flow - safer than being slower
Drive the posted work-zone speed regardless of others
Speed up to clear the zone faster
Use the shoulder to maintain normal speed
questions.explanation: Always drive at or below the posted work-zone speed - Utah law forbids exceeding posted limits even when matching traffic. Doubled fines apply. Handbook p. 49, 68.

questions.question: You see a diamond-shaped orange sign with black symbols ahead. What does this most likely mean?
choices: A general warning unrelated to construction
Work zone or construction ahead - slow down
questions.explanation: Orange + black = work zone / construction. Slow down and watch for workers, cones, and lane shifts. Handbook p. 59.

questions.question: A school bus ahead has its alternating flashing RED lights on and the stop arm extended. You must:
choices: Slow down and pass carefully on the left
Stop immediately before reaching the bus
Stop only if children are visible
questions.explanation: Alternating flashing red lights + stop arm = STOP immediately before reaching the bus. Handbook p. 46.

questions.question: On a 2-lane road, a school bus stops with red lights flashing in the oncoming direction. What must you do?
choices: Continue - you are in the opposite direction
Stop - both directions must stop on a 2-lane road
Slow down but you may proceed
Stop only if children are visible
questions.explanation: On a 2-lane road, BOTH directions must stop when a school bus is loading/unloading with red lights flashing. Handbook p. 46.

questions.question: You are northbound on a 4-lane divided highway with a wide grass median. A school bus southbound stops with red lights flashing. What must you do?
choices: Stop - all directions stop on any divided road
Continue with caution - the median exempts opposing traffic
Stop within 100 feet on either side
questions.explanation: With a physical median (4+ lanes divided), only SAME-direction vehicles must stop. Opposite-direction may proceed with caution. Handbook p. 46.

questions.question: On a 4-lane road WITHOUT a median or center turn lane, when a school bus stops with red lights flashing, who must stop?
choices: Only the lane directly next to the bus
No one - 4-lane roads exempt opposing traffic
questions.explanation: On a 4-lane road WITHOUT a median, BOTH directions must stop. The median exception only applies with a physical median or center-turn-only lane. Handbook p. 46.

questions.question: On a 5-lane road with a shared center turn lane, when a school bus has red lights flashing, who must stop?
choices: Only vehicles in the same direction as the bus
Only vehicles in the center turn lane
No one - center turn lane = exempt road
questions.explanation: 5-lane road with shared center turn lane: only SAME-direction vehicles must stop. The center turn lane acts like a median. Handbook p. 46.

questions.question: Illegally passing a school bus with red lights flashing can result in a fine of up to:
questions.explanation: Illegally passing a school bus = fine up to 3,000 dollars plus an insurance increase. Handbook p. 46.

questions.question: What is the typical posted speed limit in an active Utah school zone?
questions.explanation: School zone speed limit is 20 mph when children are present (school in session, lights flashing, or crosswalk in use). Handbook p. 53.

questions.question: It is 7:45 a.m. on a school day. You approach a school zone where the yellow lights are flashing. What does this mean?
choices: Children are present - obey the slower school-zone limit
Drive normal speed but watch carefully
Stop and call for an officer
questions.explanation: Flashing yellow school zone lights mean children are present - obey the slower school-zone speed limit (typically 20 mph). Handbook p. 91.

questions.question: An emergency vehicle with sirens and lights approaches you from behind. You must:
choices: Pull to the LEFT and stop
Pull to the RIGHT side and stop until it passes
Speed up to clear the road quickly
questions.explanation: Yield, drive immediately to the RIGHT side of the road, and STOP until the emergency vehicle has passed. Handbook p. 86.

questions.question: Utah Move Over Law requires you to slow down AND change lanes when approaching:
choices: Only police vehicles with flashing lights
Any stationary vehicle with flashing hazard lights
questions.explanation: Move Over Law applies to ANY stationary vehicle with flashing hazards adjacent to the highway - not just emergency vehicles. Handbook p. 86.

questions.question: A tow truck is stopped on the right shoulder of a 4-lane highway with amber lights flashing, hooking up a stalled car. What should you do?
choices: Drive past at normal speed - it is not an emergency vehicle
Slow down and change lanes away from the shoulder if safe
Stop completely until the tow truck leaves
Pull onto the shoulder behind it
questions.explanation: Move Over Law applies to any stationary vehicle with flashing hazards/amber lights - including tow trucks. Slow down AND change to a non-adjacent lane if practical. Handbook p. 86.

questions.question: What is the consequence if you are convicted of violating the Move Over Law and fail to attend the required defensive driving course?
choices: Court appearance with no other penalty
questions.explanation: Conviction requires a 4-hour live classroom defensive driving course within 90 days. Failing to attend = 90-day license suspension. Handbook p. 86.

questions.question: Unless you are on official business, you may not follow within how many feet of a fire vehicle responding to an alarm?
questions.explanation: Do not follow within 500 feet of a fire vehicle responding to an alarm. Handbook p. 86.

questions.question: When a fire vehicle is stopped responding to an alarm, you may not drive or park:
choices: On the same block where it is stopped
Anywhere in the same city block group
questions.explanation: You may not drive or park on the same block where a fire vehicle has stopped to answer an alarm. Handbook p. 86.

questions.question: In the HOV (express) lane, when you approach a stationary vehicle with amber lights, you should:
choices: Change lanes OUT of the HOV lane if practical
Stop and wait for the vehicle to move
questions.explanation: HOV variant of the Move Over Law: change lanes OUT of the HOV lane if practical and safe. Handbook p. 86.

questions.question: Under the Move Over Law, if you cannot safely change lanes away from a stationary vehicle with flashing hazards, you must at minimum:
choices: Slow down and provide as much space as practical
questions.explanation: If a lane change is not practical, you must still slow down and give as much room as practical. Handbook p. 86.

questions.question: You are in the HOV lane on I-15. A police car comes up behind you with lights flashing. What do you do?
choices: Stop in the HOV lane immediately
Pull to the LEFT shoulder - it is closer
Cross the double white line to the right when safe and pull to the right shoulder
Speed up to clear the HOV lane faster
questions.explanation: Pull to the RIGHT, even from the HOV lane. Utah law permits crossing the double white line out of the express lane when followed by emergency/police directing you to pull over. Handbook p. 86, 50.

questions.question: After pulling over for a traffic stop, what should you do?
choices: Get out of the car to greet the officer
Stay in the vehicle, put it in park, turn off the engine, roll down the window, and put hands on the wheel
Leave the engine running and stay in your seat
Wait until the officer knocks before doing anything
questions.explanation: Traffic stop procedure: move RIGHT, put in PARK, engine off, STAY in vehicle, window down, hands on the wheel. Handbook p. 93.

questions.question: Utah law requires headlights ON whenever you cannot clearly see people and vehicles how far ahead?
questions.explanation: Headlights must be ON from sunset to sunrise OR whenever you cannot clearly see persons/vehicles 1,000 feet ahead due to insufficient light or atmospheric conditions. Handbook p. 69.

questions.question: You must dim your high beams within how many feet of an oncoming vehicle?
questions.explanation: Dim high beams within 500 feet of an oncoming vehicle. Handbook p. 69.

questions.question: When following another vehicle at night, dim your high beams within how many feet of the vehicle ahead?
questions.explanation: Dim high beams within 300 feet when following another vehicle. (500 ft for oncoming.) Handbook p. 69.

questions.question: When using your windshield wipers continuously in rain or snow, your headlights must be:
questions.explanation: Use headlights whenever windshield wipers are in continuous use. Handbook p. 69.

questions.question: You are driving at 5 p.m. in heavy fog. Visibility ahead is about 600 feet. Should you use headlights?
choices: Yes - you cannot clearly see 1,000 feet ahead
questions.explanation: Headlights required ANY time you cannot see clearly 1,000 feet ahead - even in daylight in fog/rain/snow. Handbook p. 69.

questions.question: Should you wear sunglasses while driving at night?
choices: No - tinted lenses reduce the light you need for good vision
questions.explanation: Do NOT wear sunglasses or colored/tinted lenses at night. They reduce the light needed for safe vision. Handbook p. 70.

questions.question: How much should you add to your following distance at night compared to daytime?
questions.explanation: Increase your following distance at night by at least 2 seconds. Handbook p. 70.

questions.question: Utah requires which type of insurance on all motor vehicles?
questions.explanation: Utah requires liability insurance on all motor vehicles. Handbook p. 73.

questions.question: Drivers over 18 may use a wireless device (HWC) while driving for phone calls only if:
choices: They use voice commands to connect
The car is moving under 25 mph
The phone is held below dashboard level
They use Bluetooth headphones in both ears
questions.explanation: Drivers over 18 may talk on an HWC while driving only if they use voice commands to connect the call. Hands-free voice tech also allowed for GPS, reporting hazards/criminal activity/medical emergencies. Handbook p. 68.

questions.question: Drivers UNDER 18 may use a wireless device while driving only for which of the following?
choices: Medical emergency, safety hazard, criminal activity, or contacting a parent/guardian
Texting while stopped at a red light
questions.explanation: Under-18 drivers may only use HWC for: medical emergency, reporting safety hazard, reporting criminal activity, OR communicating with a parent/legal guardian. Handbook p. 68.

questions.question: A first-offense texting-while-driving conviction in Utah is what kind of crime, with what maximum fine?
questions.explanation: A first texting violation = Class C misdemeanor, max fine 100 dollars. Handbook p. 68.

questions.question: Under what conditions is a texting violation upgraded to a Class B misdemeanor?
choices: When it causes bodily injury OR the driver has a previous texting offense
When done at speeds over 35 mph
When the driver does not have insurance
questions.explanation: Texting becomes a Class B misdemeanor if it causes bodily injury OR the driver has a previous texting offense. Handbook p. 68.

questions.question: Texting while driving in Utah is a:
choices: Secondary offense - officer needs another reason to stop you
Primary offense - officer can pull you over just for texting
Civil infraction - no possible jail time ever
questions.explanation: Texting is a primary offense in Utah - officers can pull you over solely for observing texting. Handbook p. 68.

questions.question: While driving, Utah law generally prohibits which of the following?
choices: Manual data entry into a wireless device (texting, dialing, browsing, etc.)
questions.explanation: Utah law prohibits manual interaction with a handheld wireless device while driving - texting, dialing, browsing, photographing, watching video, etc. Handbook p. 67-68.

questions.question: You are 16 and driving. Your mom calls. Can you legally answer using voice-activated hands-free?
choices: No - under 18 cannot use a phone for any reason while driving
Yes - communicating with a parent/guardian is an allowed exception
Only if you pull over first
Only if you are going under 25 mph
questions.explanation: Under-18 drivers may use HWC to communicate with a parent/legal guardian - one of the four allowed exceptions. Handbook p. 68.

questions.question: Above what trailer weight does Utah law require brakes on the trailer?
questions.explanation: Trailers over 2,000 lbs require brakes in Utah. (Over 750 lbs is when brakes are recommended.) Handbook p. 98.

questions.question: Air bags are designed to:
choices: Work WITH safety belts, not as a replacement
Inflate only in crashes over 60 mph
questions.explanation: Air bags work WITH safety belts - not as a replacement. You still need to be buckled in. Handbook p. 39.

questions.question: Air bags deploy at speeds up to about how many mph?
questions.explanation: Air bags deploy at up to 200 mph - drivers/front passengers should be as far back from the bag as practical. Handbook p. 39.

questions.question: An infant in a rear-facing seat may never ride:
choices: In the front seat with an active passenger air bag
On a long trip without stops
In a vehicle with side-curtain air bags
questions.explanation: Infants must NEVER ride in the front seat with an active passenger-side air bag. The bag can cause fatal injuries to a rear-facing infant. Handbook p. 39.

questions.question: Disabling, modifying, or removing your vehicle air bag system is:
choices: Allowed if it is your own car
An infraction with a 25 dollar fine
questions.explanation: Disabling or modifying the air bag passive restraint system = Class B misdemeanor. Handbook p. 39.

questions.question: Headlights are required from:
choices: Sunset to sunrise (or whenever visibility is reduced below 1,000 ft)
Only when other cars have lights on
questions.explanation: Headlights required from sunset to sunrise OR any time you cannot see 1,000 ft ahead due to insufficient light/conditions. Handbook p. 69.

questions.question: It is 9 p.m. on a 2-lane highway. You are using high beams. A car is approaching from the opposite direction, about 600 feet away. What do you do?
choices: Keep high beams - the other driver should dim first
Dim your high beams now - you must dim within 500 ft
Flash your high beams as a signal
questions.explanation: Dim your high beams within 500 feet of an oncoming vehicle - at 600 ft you should be preparing to dim. Do not wait for the other driver. Handbook p. 69.

questions.question: You are 22 years old. While driving, you want to start GPS navigation. What is legal?
choices: Type the destination by hand while driving - adults can use phones freely
Use voice activation to start the GPS
Hold the phone in your hand and tap once at a red light
Only passengers may touch the phone
questions.explanation: Adults (18+) may use hands-free voice commands for GPS, calls, and certain alerts. Manual entry while driving is prohibited. Handbook p. 67-68.

questions.question: In Utah, everyone in a moving vehicle must:
choices: Have a fastened safety belt or be in an approved child seat
questions.explanation: Everyone in the vehicle must have a fastened safety belt or be in a child seat. Handbook p. 37.

questions.question: When can an officer stop a vehicle solely for a seat-belt violation?
questions.explanation: An officer can stop you solely if anyone under 16 is unrestrained (primary offense). 16+ occupants are cited only if stopped for another reason (secondary). Handbook p. 38.

questions.question: Which is a proper seat-belt fit?
choices: Shoulder belt under the arm or behind the back
Shoulder belt across the shoulder/chest, lap belt across the hips
Shoulder belt clipped to keep it slack
questions.explanation: Shoulder belt across the shoulder/chest; lap belt across the hips (not the stomach). Never put the shoulder belt under your arm or behind your back. Handbook p. 37.

questions.question: Smoking in a vehicle while transporting a minor age 15 or younger is:
questions.explanation: Smoking while transporting children 15 and younger is an infraction. Handbook p. 39.

questions.question: Where should children 12 and younger ride?
choices: Wherever there is a free seat
questions.explanation: Children 12 and younger should ride in the back seat. Handbook p. 38.

questions.question: Utah law requires children to use a child or booster seat until age:
questions.explanation: Children must use a child/booster seat until they are 8 years old OR 57 inches tall - whichever comes first. Handbook p. 37-38.

questions.question: Children should ride rear-facing in the back seat until at least:
questions.explanation: Rear-facing in the back seat until at least 2 years and 30 lbs (longer if the seat allows). Handbook p. 38.

questions.question: After rear-facing, children should ride forward-facing with a harness until at least:
questions.explanation: Forward-facing with harness until at least 4 years and 40 lbs. Handbook p. 38.

questions.question: A booster seat should be used until the child is:
choices: 57 inches tall and the belt fits properly
60 inches tall regardless of belt fit
questions.explanation: Booster seat from approximately 4 years / 40 lbs until the seat belt fits properly AND the child is 57 inches tall. Handbook p. 38.

questions.question: For proper driving position, how much space should be between your chest and the steering wheel?
questions.explanation: Sit with at least 10 inches between your chest and the steering wheel. Protects you from air bag injury. Handbook p. 37.

questions.question: Your headrest should contact the back of your head:
choices: Above the level of your ears
questions.explanation: The headrest should contact the back of your head ABOVE the level of your ears to prevent whiplash. Handbook p. 37.

questions.question: To set the LEFT side mirror correctly, you should:
choices: Sit straight and align the mirror so you see most of your car side
Rest your head against the left window and adjust so you barely see the rear left edge of your car
Tilt the mirror down toward the ground
Mirror position does not matter - use blind spot only
questions.explanation: Rest your head against the closed left window and set the mirror so you can BARELY see the rear left edge of your vehicle. Reduces blind spots. Handbook p. 37.

questions.question: How do you set the RIGHT side mirror properly?
choices: Same way as the left mirror
Lean your head right (under the rearview mirror) and adjust so you barely see the right side of your car
Tilt to point at the rear seat area
Aim it at the rear bumper
questions.explanation: Lean right so your head is directly below the rearview mirror, then set the right mirror so you can BARELY see the right side of your vehicle. Handbook p. 37.

questions.question: When backing your vehicle, you should primarily:
choices: Turn your body, place your right arm on the seat back, and look through the rear window
Steer with one hand and look at side mirrors only
questions.explanation: Mirrors do not show directly behind you. Turn, put your right arm on the seat back, and look through the rear window. Check behind the vehicle BEFORE getting in. Handbook p. 40.

questions.question: Where is backing up your vehicle prohibited in Utah?
questions.explanation: Do NOT back up on a freeway or interstate. If you miss your exit, continue to the next one. Handbook p. 40.

questions.question: Your car has front and side air bags. You only put on the lap belt and skip the shoulder belt. Are the air bags enough?
choices: Yes - the air bags do the same job
No - air bags work WITH safety belts, not instead of them
Yes - if you sit far back from the wheel
questions.explanation: Air bags work WITH safety belts, not as a replacement. Without the belt, the air bag can injure you. Wear both. Handbook p. 39.

questions.question: Your friend does not have insurance and asks to borrow your car for a quick run. What is true?
choices: Insurance follows the driver, not the car
Utah requires liability insurance on the vehicle - your insurance generally covers permissive users
Insurance is optional if the driver is over 18
Friends are automatically covered by your insurance
questions.explanation: Utah requires liability insurance on all motor vehicles. The vehicle must be insured; the policy generally covers permissive users - check with your insurer. Handbook p. 73.

questions.question: You must not park within how many feet of a fire hydrant?
questions.explanation: No parking within 15 feet of a fire hydrant. Handbook p. 42.

questions.question: You must not park within how many feet of a crosswalk at an intersection?
questions.explanation: No parking on a crosswalk or within 20 feet of one. Handbook p. 42.

questions.question: You must not park within how many feet of a stop sign, yield sign, traffic signal, or flashing beacon?
questions.explanation: No parking within 30 feet of a flashing beacon, stop sign, yield sign, or traffic signal. Handbook p. 42.

questions.question: You must not park within how many feet of the nearest rail of a railroad crossing?
questions.explanation: Never park within 50 feet of the nearest rail (and never park ON a railroad track). Handbook p. 42.

questions.question: When parallel parked, your vehicle back wheel should be no more than how far from the curb?
questions.explanation: Parked vehicles must be no more than 12 inches from the curb. Handbook p. 42-43.

questions.question: You spot a curbside parking space: 18 feet from a fire hydrant and 25 feet from a stop sign. Is parking there legal?
choices: No - 18 feet from hydrant is too close
No - 25 feet from stop sign violates the 30-foot rule
No - both distances violate Utah law
questions.explanation: 18 feet from the hydrant is OK (15+ rule). But 25 feet from the stop sign violates the 30-foot rule. Find another space. Handbook p. 42.

questions.question: You must not park within how many feet of a fire station driveway?
questions.explanation: 20 feet from a fire station driveway (and 75 feet on the opposite side if signs are posted). Handbook p. 42.

questions.question: When parking DOWNHILL with a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: It does not matter if the parking brake is set
questions.explanation: Downhill with a curb: turn wheels TOWARD the curb so a rolling car catches on the curb. Handbook p. 42.

questions.question: When parking UPHILL with a curb, you should turn your front wheels:
choices: Wheels do not matter - only the parking brake
questions.explanation: Uphill with a curb: turn wheels AWAY from the curb so a rolling car backs INTO the curb instead of into traffic. Handbook p. 42.

questions.question: When parking UPHILL with NO curb, you should turn your front wheels:
questions.explanation: Uphill with NO curb: turn wheels to the RIGHT - a rolling car drifts off the road, not into traffic. Handbook p. 42.

questions.question: Maya is parking on a steep downhill street with a curb on her right. What is the correct wheel position?
choices: Does not matter if she sets the parking brake
questions.explanation: Downhill with curb on the right = wheels toward the curb (RIGHT). Set the parking brake. Handbook p. 42.

questions.question: Before leaving a parked vehicle, you should:
choices: Set the parking brake, stop the motor, and lock the doors
Just turn off the engine - that is enough
Roll down windows to vent the cabin
Leave the engine running if you will be brief
questions.explanation: Before leaving: set the parking brake, stop the motor, lock the doors. Handbook p. 42.

questions.question: On a narrow mountain road where two vehicles cannot pass, who must yield?
choices: The uphill driver - they have momentum to lose
The downhill driver - they have brakes and can back up easier
questions.explanation: On narrow mountain roads, the DOWNHILL driver must yield to the UPHILL driver. Counterintuitive - uphill has more difficulty restarting. Handbook p. 69.

questions.question: You are driving DOWN a narrow mountain road. A vehicle is coming UP and you cannot both pass. What should you do?
choices: Hold your line - downhill has gravity priority
Yield to the uphill driver - back up if needed
Both drivers stop and wait for help
questions.explanation: Downhill yields to uphill - back up to a wider spot if needed. The uphill driver has a harder time starting again. Handbook p. 69.

questions.question: When driving DOWNHILL on a steep mountain road, you should:
choices: Coast in neutral to save fuel
Use a low gear and proper braking
Use cruise control to maintain a constant speed
Disengage the clutch to ease the engine
questions.explanation: Driving downhill: pick a safe speed, use proper braking, and use a LOW gear. Do NOT coast in neutral or disengage the clutch - you lose engine braking. Handbook p. 69.

questions.question: When driving UPHILL on a steep grade, you should:
choices: Stay in the LEFT lane to keep momentum
Use lower gears; stay in the right lane if you are slow
Disengage the clutch to keep engine RPMs up
Tailgate the vehicle ahead to draft
questions.explanation: Driving uphill: use lower gears. If slow, pull off or stay in the right lane. Handbook p. 69.

questions.question: Hydroplaning generally begins at speeds over:
questions.explanation: Hydroplaning starts at speeds over 35 mph - your front tires ride on water instead of the road. Handbook p. 70.

questions.question: You are on a wet road at 50 mph. What is the safest move to reduce hydroplaning risk?
choices: Drive at the posted speed regardless of conditions
Slow down - hydroplaning starts above 35 mph
Tap the brakes constantly to clear water
questions.explanation: Hydroplaning starts above 35 mph. Slow down on wet roads - posted speeds may be too fast in heavy rain. Handbook p. 70.

questions.question: How much moving water is enough to cause loss of control or stalling for most passenger cars?
questions.explanation: 6 inches of water reaches the bottom of most passenger cars - enough to cause loss of control or stalling. Handbook p. 67.

questions.question: How much moving water can carry away most vehicles, including SUVs and pickups?
questions.explanation: 2 feet of moving water can carry away most vehicles, including SUVs and pickup trucks. Handbook p. 67.

questions.question: You approach a flooded section of road. The water looks shallow but moving. What is the correct response?
choices: Drive through slowly - your SUV can handle it
Turn around - never cross a flooded roadway
Stop in the water and wait for it to recede
questions.explanation: Turn around, do not drown. Never cross a flooded roadway - depth is hard to judge, and 6 inches stalls cars, 2 feet carries them away. Handbook p. 67.

questions.question: Black ice on Utah roadways:
choices: Appears as a bright white patch - easy to spot
Looks like a wet spot - hard to see
questions.explanation: Black ice looks like a wet spot, not white ice - that is why it is so dangerous. Common Utah hazard, especially on bridges and shaded areas. Handbook p. 70.

questions.question: An animal runs into the road in front of you. What is the safest response?
choices: Swerve hard to avoid it - animals first
Do not swerve - keep the vehicle under control in your lane and brake firmly
Speed up to pass before it crosses
Honk and flash lights but do not slow
questions.explanation: Do NOT swerve for animals - keep the vehicle under control in your lane. Swerving causes more crashes than the animal would. Handbook p. 66.

questions.question: In Utah, brakes are required on trailers over what weight?
questions.explanation: Trailers over 2,000 lbs require brakes in Utah. Over 750 lbs is the recommended threshold. Handbook p. 98.

questions.question: On a narrow mountain road approaching a sharp curve with limited visibility, you should:
choices: Maintain speed to clear the curve quickly
Sound your horn to alert oncoming traffic
questions.explanation: Sound the horn approaching sharp curves on narrow mountain roads where view is obstructed. Handbook p. 69.

questions.question: If caught in a lightning storm in your vehicle, you should:
choices: Exit the vehicle and lie flat on the ground
Stay inside a HARD-TOPPED vehicle with doors closed and windows up, avoid touching metal
Use the radio to call for help
questions.explanation: Only a hard-topped vehicle protects you in lightning. Doors closed, windows up, do NOT touch metal surfaces or use a radio with an external antenna. Handbook p. 69.

questions.question: On a narrow or single-lane bridge, who has the right-of-way?
choices: The first driver to reach the bridge
questions.explanation: On narrow/single-lane bridges, the FIRST driver to the bridge has the right-of-way. Take turns crossing. Handbook p. 70.

questions.question: Before a long trip, you should check which of these vehicle components?
choices: Only the gas tank - that is enough
Windshield/windows, lights, tires, fluids, belts, hoses, brakes
questions.explanation: Pre-trip inspection: windshield/windows, lights, tires, fluid levels, belts, hoses, brakes. Plus get a good night sleep. Handbook p. 71.

questions.question: If your vehicle enters a body of water, it will typically float for how long?
questions.explanation: A submerged vehicle floats for 30-60 seconds. Get out immediately: remove seat belt, lower window, climb out the top. Handbook p. 71.

questions.question: Your car has entered a lake and is starting to sink. In what order should you escape?
choices: Call 911, then try the door, then break the window
Remove seat belt, lower the window, climb out the top
Try to restart the engine to drive out
Wait until it stops sinking, then push the door open
questions.explanation: Submerged escape order: remove seat belt, then lower the window, then climb onto the vehicle top, then assess current. Keep shoes on. Handbook p. 71.

questions.question: Two lanes are merging into one because of a construction closure. Traffic in both lanes is stopped or slow. What's the right way to merge?
choices: Both lanes squeeze right immediately so the left lane is empty before the merge point
Both lanes stay full until the merge point and then take turns one-by-one (zipper merge)
The left lane must yield to the right lane the whole way
questions.explanation: Zipper merge: both lanes stay full until the merge point, then take turns one car from each lane. Utah law backs this — moving over early actually slows everyone down.
